content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • •  Administration • •  Executive/Legal • •  Enforcement
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901          322-6441

February 15, 1985


Michael Paparian, Director
Sierra Club Legislative Office
1228 N Street, Ste. 31
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Our File No. A-84-305

Dear Mr. Paparian:

     I apologize for the delay in responding to your letter of
December 7, 1984, requesting advice with respect to the lobbying
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (Government
Code Sections 81000-91014).

     As outlined in your letter, the Sierra Club plans to
consolidate its individual lobbyist checking accounts into one
account which will be used by all of the Club's lobbyists.  The
purpose of the account is to reimburse and make advances to the
Club's lobbyists for expenses, and for general overhead and
operating expenses incurred by the Sacramento legislative office.
Initially, the national Sierra Club office in San Francisco will
advance funds to the account and will reimburse the account when
it receives documentation of payments made.

     During our telephone conversation of January 30, 1985, I
recommended that payments made from the account be reported in
the following manner:  All payments will be reported as if they
were made directly by the lobbyist employer.  That is, each
lobbyist will report the amount he or she receives for reim-
bursements and advances in the appropriate column of Part II.
(Payments Received in Connection with Lobbyist Activity) of the Lobbyist
Report (Form 620).  The same information will be reported in the
appropriate column of Part II.A. (Individual Lobbyists Employed)
of the Lobbyist Employer Report (Form 650).  In addition, all
payments made from the account for overhead and operating costs
for the Sacramento legislative office will be reported as "Other
Payments" in Part II.D.2. of the Lobbyist Employer Report.  The
lobbyists are not required to report these payments on their
Lobbyist Reports.

Michael Paparian
February 15, 1985
Page 2


     I hope this information is helpful.  Please let me know if
you have any further questions.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Carla Wardlow
                                        Political Reform Consultant

SIERRA CLUB

LEGISLATIVE OFFICE

1228 N Street, Suite 31
Sacramento, California 95814
(916) 444-6906

by Ansel Adams


December 7, 1984

Carla Wardlow
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K St.
Sacramento, CA 95814


Dear Ms. Wardlow:

     Thank you again for your advice regarding changing our
checking account and accounting systems in our office. So that
there is no confusion about the legality of our new accounting
practices as they relate to the Political Reform Act, I would
appreciate it if you or another representative of the FPPC would
indicate in writing whether my understanding as outlined below is
correct.

     As I understand the requirements, it is acceptable for us to
do the following:

     1. Close the existing checking accounts in the names of the
        individual lobbyists in our office which are used to keep
        a record of expenses. Replace the existing accounts with
        a single account for our office in Sacramento. The single
        account would be in the name "Sierra Club."

     2. Deposit an "advance" into our Sacramento account from our
        national office in San Francisco (the "lobbyist
        employer").

     3. Report the "advance" as an "advance" on the lobbying
        report (form 620) of the director of our Sacramento
        Office.

     4. Allow any of the lobbyists or other employees in the
        office to sign checks from the account.

     5. Pay expenses incurred by our office (phone, copying, etc.)
        from the account.

Carla Wardlow
12/7/84
Page Two


6. Reimburse the lobbyists in the office for personally
   incurred expenses from the account.

7. Periodically request our national office in San Francisco
   to make out a check to Sierra Club to be deposited into
   our account in Sacramento. The amount of the check would
   be equal to all expenses and reimbursements paid for from
   the account. The request would be substantiated with
   appropriate documentation.

8. With the exception of the office director, the lobbyists
   will report on their forms 620 the amounts they are
   reimbursed from the office account plus any additional
   amounts paid for from personal funds which must be
   reported pursuant to the Political Reform Act. The office
   director will report the balance of the funds expended
   the account on his form 620 plus any of his personal
   expenses which must be reported.

9. For the purposes of complying with the political reform
   act, the the monies described in points 7 and 8 above can
   be reported on form 620 as either "reimbursements" (column
   b on page 1) or "advances" (column c on page 1).

10. The lobbyists will inform the Sierra Club national office
    (the lobbyist employer) of the amounts described in point
    8 above which are related to the office account. These
    figures will be used by the national office in filing out
    their form 650.

I appreciate your attention to this matter.


Sincerely,

Michael Paparian
Director

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

December 14, 1984


Michael Paparian
Director
Sierra Club
1228 N Street, Suite 31
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: A-84-305

Dear Mr. Paparian:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Diane Maura Fishburn, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
General Counsel

BAM:plh

sections.question: As I understand the requirements, it is acceptable for us to do the following: 1. Close the existing checking accounts in the names of the individual lobbyists in our office which are used to keep a record of expenses. Replace the existing accounts with a single account for our office in Sacramento. The single account would be in the name "Sierra Club." 2. Deposit an "advance" into our Sacramento account from our national office in San Francisco (the "lobbyist employer"). 3. Report the "advance" as an "advance" on the lobbying report (form 620) of the director of our Sacramento Office. 4. Allow any of the lobbyists or other employees in the office to sign checks from the account. 5. Pay expenses incurred by our office (phone, copying, etc.) from the account. 6. Reimburse the lobbyists in the office for personally incurred expenses from the account. 7. Periodically request our national office in San Francisco to make out a check to Sierra Club to be deposited into our account in Sacramento. The amount of the check would be equal to all expenses and reimbursements paid for from the account. The request would be substantiated with appropriate documentation. 8. With the exception of the office director, the lobbyists will report on their forms 620 the amounts they are reimbursed from the office account plus any additional amounts paid for from personal funds which must be reported pursuant to the Political Reform Act. The office director will report the balance of the funds expended from the account on his form 620 plus any of his personal expenses which must be reported. 9. For the purposes of complying with the political reform act, the monies described in points 7 and 8 above can be reported on form 620 as either "reimbursements" (column b on page 1) or "advances" (column c on page 1). 10. The lobbyists will inform the Sierra Club national office (the lobbyist employer) of the amounts described in point 8 above which are related to the office account. These figures will be used by the national office in filling out their form 650.
sections.conclusion: All payments will be reported as if they were "made directly by the lobbyist employer." That is, each lobbyist will report the amount he or she receives for reimbursements and advances in the appropriate column of Part II. (Payments Received in Connection with Lobbying) of the Lobbyist Report (Form 620). The same information will be reported in the appropriate column of Part II.A. (Individual Lobbyists Employed) of the Lobbyist Employer Report (Form 650). In addition, all payments made from the account for overhead and operating costs for the Sacramento legislative office will be reported as "Other Payments" in Part II.D.2. of the Lobbyist Employer Report. The lobbyists are not required to report these payments on their Lobbyist Reports.
sections.question_synthetic: The Sierra Club is consolidating individual lobbyist checking accounts into a single Sacramento office account and seeks FPPC confirmation that their proposed accounting and reporting practices comply with the Political Reform Act's lobbying disclosure provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC approved the consolidation of accounts with specific reporting requirements: lobbyists must report reimbursements and advances on Form 620, the same information must appear on the employer's Form 650, and overhead/operating costs must be reported as "Other Payments" on the employer report only.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (December 7, 1984) and FPPC response (February 15, 1985). Initial referral letter (December 14, 1984) also included. All sections clearly identifiable. Date discrepancy: document ID year is 1984 but response letter is dated February 15, 1985.

QUESTION: As I understand the requirements, it is acceptable for us to do the following: 1. Close the existing checking accounts in the names of the individual lobbyists in our office which are used to keep a record of expenses. Replace the existing accounts with a single account for our office in Sacramento. The single account would be in the name "Sierra Club." 2. Deposit an "advance" into our Sacramento account from our national office in San Francisco (the "lobbyist employer"). 3. Report the "advance" as an "advance" on the lobbying report (form 620) of the director of our Sacramento Office. 4. Allow any of the lobbyists or other employees in the office to sign checks from the account. 5. Pay expenses incurred by our office (phone, copying, etc.) from the account. 6. Reimburse the lobbyists in the office for personally incurred expenses from the account. 7. Periodically request our national office in San Francisco to make out a check to Sierra Club to be deposited into our account in Sacramento. The amount of the check would be equal to all expenses and reimbursements paid for from the account. The request would be substantiated with appropriate documentation. 8. With the exception of the office director, the lobbyists will report on their forms 620 the amounts they are reimbursed from the office account plus any additional amounts paid for from personal funds which must be reported pursuant to the Political Reform Act. The office director will report the balance of the funds expended from the account on his form 620 plus any of his personal expenses which must be reported. 9. For the purposes of complying with the political reform act, the monies described in points 7 and 8 above can be reported on form 620 as either "reimbursements" (column b on page 1) or "advances" (column c on page 1). 10. The lobbyists will inform the Sierra Club national office (the lobbyist employer) of the amounts described in point 8 above which are related to the office account. These figures will be used by the national office in filling out their form 650.

CONCLUSION: All payments will be reported as if they were "made directly by the lobbyist employer." That is, each lobbyist will report the amount he or she receives for reimbursements and advances in the appropriate column of Part II. (Payments Received in Connection with Lobbying) of the Lobbyist Report (Form 620). The same information will be reported in the appropriate column of Part II.A. (Individual Lobbyists Employed) of the Lobbyist Employer Report (Form 650). In addition, all payments made from the account for overhead and operating costs for the Sacramento legislative office will be reported as "Other Payments" in Part II.D.2. of the Lobbyist Employer Report. The lobbyists are not required to report these payments on their Lobbyist Reports.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to Sierra Club's inquiry regarding consolidation of individual lobbyist checking accounts into a single Sacramento office account, providing detailed guidance on how to properly report account transactions on lobbying disclosure forms.
